005</li><li>Complete two Introductory courses: ANTH 001, ANTH 005, BIO 011, COGS 005, ECON 001, PHIL 001, POLI 001, **PSY 001 and SOC 001</li><li>Complete one introductory computing course: BIOE 021 OR CSE 005 OR CSE 019</li></ul><p><br></p><p>* If using MATH 011 or MATH 021 to satisfy the Admissions requirement, the course also fulfills the Quantitative Reasoning Requirement.</p><p>** If using Visit <a href=\"https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements</a> for more specific UC Merced admissions information.</p><p><br></p><p>Prior to Transferring to UC Merced, please be advised of the following for Junior Transfers:</p><p><br></p><p><strong>1) WRI 001 and WRI 010 are admissions requirements:</strong> WRI 001 is fulfilled by IGETC/Cal-GETC 1A - English Composition. WRI 010 is fulfilled by IGETC/Cal-GETC 1B - Critical Thinking/English Composition. Please scroll towards the bottom of the agreement to find the articulation for each course.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>2) Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG):</strong> UC Merced is one of the six UC's that offers Transfer Admissions Guarantee. Please visit the TAG website for more information: <a href=\"https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag</a></p><p><br></p><p><strong>3) General Education (GE Requirements):</strong> While general education is not required for admission, it can help speed up your time to graduation once you transfer to UC Merced. We highly recommend reviewing the <a href=\"https://catalog.ucmerced.edu/content.php?catoid=22&navoid=2163#transfer\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Transfer Students: Satisfying General Education</a> page in the catalog for a more extensive explanation of the requirements. Transfer students are strongly encouraged to complete IGETC in order to prepare for work within the School of Social Sciences, Humanities and Arts (SSHA). IGETC courses may double count with the major courses listed below. Please visit your Community College Counselor to learn more.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>4. This agreement displays all lower-division (or Major Preparation) courses required in the major.</strong> UC Merced courses on the left, approved (articulated) transfer courses to the right.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>5. Changes to this Agreements:</strong> Major requirements are subject to change from one academic year to the next. Newly-articulated courses are added on a rolling basis, and articulated courses can be revised. Visit ASSIST every semester for the latest information and consult with an Academic Counselor at your institution on a regular basis.</p><p><br></p><p>For more questions about admissions, please
